---
id: wallet-operation-details
title: "Get a single wallet operation by id"
description: "Returns a single wallet operation by its `operation_id`, together with every ledger entry it produced. An operation is the atomic unit of work the wallet performs — a reservation, a refund, a deduction, an adjustment, or a reversal — and rolls up one or more ledger entries under one identifier."

import RequestSchema from "@theme/RequestSchema";

<Heading
  as={"h1"}
  className={"openapi__heading"}
  children={"Get a single wallet operation by id"}
>
</Heading>

<MethodEndpoint
  method={"post"}
  path={"/b/wallet/ottu/v1/operations/"}
  context={"endpoint"}
>
  
</MethodEndpoint>



Returns a single wallet operation by its `operation_id`, together with every ledger entry it produced. An operation is the atomic unit of work the wallet performs — a reservation, a refund, a deduction, an adjustment, or a reversal — and rolls up one or more ledger entries under one identifier.

Use this when you have an operation ID from an earlier response — for example the operation returned by a [refund to wallet](/developers/operations/#refund-to-wallet) — and you want the canonical record for audit or reconciliation. A reservation operation, for instance, produces a `reserve` entry that is later closed by a `debit_payment` entry from the deduction operation; both are returned here.

  id={"request"}
  as={"h2"}
